summary: This brief post shares a positive review of CityDesk from the Sydney Morning Herald, which praises it as the best answer for small-to-medium sites transitioning to content management systems. Joel also references a repeated interview with Rick Chapman, reaffirming his longstanding position against rewriting large software products from scratch. The post reinforces two recurring Spolsky themes: promoting his own product and opposing ground-up rewrites.
key_points: CityDesk is praised by the Sydney Morning Herald as the best solution for small-to-medium sites needing a content management system
Joel reaffirms his well-known stance against rewriting large software products from scratch, even for products as problematic as Outlook
The 'Flogging of Dead Horses' label suggests Joel is aware he keeps repeating this anti-rewrite argument but stands firmly by it
money_quotes: I still don't think you should rewrite large software products from the ground up. Not even Outlook.
key_insight: Joel remains unwavering in his conviction that rewriting large software from scratch is a mistake, no matter how flawed the existing codebase.
